issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition properties (line 443), causing spatial movement on state changes. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who require reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size (WCAG 2.5.5). While this meets the minimum 24px requirement, it may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 436:   min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, lacking a visible focus indicator replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which falls below the 44px recommended target size. This smaller button variant may be difficult for some users to activate without error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 456:   min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommendation. More critically, at 28px it is smaller than the recommended 44px and may cause activation difficulties.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 586:   width: 44px;
    Line 587:   height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input

issuesFound.title: Touch target at minimum size without padding compensation on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size. No additional padding is provided in the same rule to extend the touch area to 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1428:   min-height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The ::after pseudo-element on feature card links has a transition on the transform property, creating a sliding arrow animation. No prefers-reduced-motion media query overrides this specific transition.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item class transitions the transform property for a hover lift effect. There is no corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial animation for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on price period text
issuesFound.explanation: The .price-period class uses colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is provided in this rule-block. Keyboard users cannot see focus on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 434: outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The blockquote element uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background. This produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text. Users with low vision will have difficulty reading this content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 316: blockquote { border-left: 4px solid var(--colour-border-brand); margin: 0; padding-left: var(--space-6); font-style: italic; color: var(--colour-neutral-600); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector suppresses the default focus outline with outline: none and outline-offset but fails to provide a replacement visual indicator such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout and fails to hide it visually while keeping it accessible. More critically, it lacks the absolute positioning and clipping necessary to ensure screen readers can access the content while sighted users cannot, and it may cause layout issues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit dimensions of 28px by 28px with zero padding, creating a touch target well below the 44px recommendation and only marginally above the 24px minimum. Users may struggle to activate this button on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets a min-height of 32px, which falls below the 44px recommended target size for touch inputs. While this meets the minimum 24px requirement, users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button without accidentally hitting adjacent elements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm modifier sets min-height: 36px and vertical padding of only 6px (var(--space-1-5)), which does not compensate to reach the recommended 44px touch target size. This makes the small button variant difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which falls below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor disabilities may find it impossible to activate the checkbox without hitting adjacent targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but fails the recommended 44px touch target size. The small hit area makes precise activation difficult for users with limited motor control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2310: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies a translateY(1px) transform on :active. This spatial movement animation is not disabled under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), which can trigger vestibular disorders in users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Users in high-contrast mode will not see focus indicators, making keyboard navigation impossible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring because the outline width is zero. Keyboard users will have no visible indication of which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no compensating padding, resulting in a touch target at the minimum 24px size but below the recommended 44px. This may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will not see when the search input has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle min-height/min-width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button has an explicit size of 28px with padding of 6px (var(--space-2)), resulting in a total touch area of approximately 40px. This falls below the 44px recommended target size and may cause accidental activations or misses.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets explicit minimum dimensions of 20px by 20px, falling below the 24px minimum required for accessible touch targets. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a/button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu items have a min-height of 28px with vertical padding of 6px, creating a total height of approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height of 28px with vertical padding of 6px, creating a total height of approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links set outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px. No visible focus indicator is provided, preventing keyboard users from tracking their position within the submenu.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. Without a visible outline, border, or box-shadow, keyboard users cannot perceive focus on this button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. This removes the focus ring without providing a visible alternative, making buttons inaccessible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets an explicit min-height of 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users may struggle to toggle this checkbox accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links have a min-height of 28px with vertical padding of 7.5px, resulting in a total height of approximately 43px. This is just below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px by 28px with zero padding, resulting in a touch target of only 28px. This is well below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible. This utility class does not properly hide content from visual display but keeps it for screen readers as intended; instead, it makes content visible.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause spatial movement animations. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition property for hover effects. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists for this specific button transition, which may trigger motion sensitivity.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties that creates spatial movement on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1256 is empty and does not override this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .feature-card a::after
issuesFound.explanation: The feature card link arrow uses transform transition for horizontal movement on hover. This spatial animation is not covered by the existing prefers-reduced-motion block (which only targets .feature-card:hover), leaving users vulnerable to motion triggers.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: The product card includes transform in its transition for hover elevation effects. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists for this component, which may cause issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not render a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px with no padding, meeting the minimum but falling short of the 44px recommended target size without compensatory spacing. This creates a cramped touch target.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible only sets outline-offset: -3px without restoring outline width or style. The outline remains none (inherited from the global rule), leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a

issuesFound.fix: Line 703: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Button active state transform lacks reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies transform: translateY(1px) for a pressed effect. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 626: Add after line 626: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Checkbox touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The input[type="checkbox"] selector sets min-height: 20px (and width: 20px), creating a touch target below the 24px minimum. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to toggle checkboxes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1059: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 236: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient footer address link colour contrast

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for Windows High Contrast users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not render a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. The border-radius property does not provide visibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform transition for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class inherits outline: none from the global rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without restoring a visible outline style or providing a box-shadow replacement.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, which is below the 44px recommended size and has no compensating padding to meet the 44px target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the default state, links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 188: a {\n  text-decoration: underline;\n} or alternatively add border-bottom to links: border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The selectors #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and #nav-menu > li > button:focus-visible set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, removing the focus ring without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with outline-offset: -3px but does not provide a visible replacement indicator like box-shadow or border.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .feature-card a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, removing the focus ring without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tab buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .filter-tabs button:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on inline code elements
issuesFound.explanation: The code element uses color: var(--colour-brand-secondary) (#bb88ee) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.9:1, which fails the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, leaving no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .footer-brand a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, providing no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.8:1

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which does not hide content visually. It fails to implement the screen-reader-only technique, potentially exposing helper text to sighted users or failing to hide it if intended.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion) block at Line 609 is empty and does not disable this motion, which can affect users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA buttons have min-height 32px, below the 44px recommended size. Primary actions should be easy to touch.

issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand > p text color (#94a3b8) on dark background (#555555) has extremely low contrast (1.6:1), making it unreadable for many users.

issuesFound.fix: Line 169: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The base :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements that do not override this rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function links an error message to a field using aria-describedby, but it fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on this attribute to identify invalid fields to users, so its absence violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 920: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function, before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function updates the visual state and CSS classes but fails to set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1115: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment within the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` within the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the function manages the panel visibility and animations but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen readers unaware that the answer panel is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` within the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never created or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation module declares a focus trap variable (_trap) and deactivates it on close, but it never instantiates createFocusTrap() or activates it when the menu opens. Keyboard users can tab out of the open drawer and lose focus context, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1130 (inside init function, after _initialised = true): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger, escapeDeactivates: true });` and activate it in the open function at Line 1115 by adding `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The accessibility state remains out of sync with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger click handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick handler toggles the dropdown visibility but does not announce the state change (open/close) to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT may not know the menu state changed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. Assistive technology will not reflect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onAccept handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept function saves preferences and closes the banner but does not announce the success message to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their choice was saved.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _onDecline handler lacks live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onDecline function saves preferences and closes the banner but does not announce the success message to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their choice was saved.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1252: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the null check within the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes to display the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2085: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` within the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but does not announce the closure to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu has closed, especially if they activated the close button via keyboard.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2112: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` before the emit call at the end of the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2274: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` within the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Neither the open nor close functions announce the visibility change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technologies will not be informed when a dropdown menu opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2277 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after the panel is shown. Line 2290 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the code animates the panel opening and updates CSS classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the disclosure pattern and prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3156: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` within the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a `_trap` variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap(). When the menu opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger, leaving keyboard users disoriented.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2087 (in mobileNav.open): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` followed by `_trap.activate();` after the drawer is shown. Line 2102 (in mobileNav.close): Change `_trap && _trap.isActive()` to `_trap?.deactivate();` ensuring the trap exists.

issuesFound.title: createFocusTrap focus return lacks fallback
issuesFound.explanation: When deactivating the focus trap, if the saved focus element (_savedFocus) is no longer in the DOM, focus is not programmatically moved anywhere, causing it to be lost to the document body. This disorients keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1185: Replace the focus restoration block with: `if (_savedFocus && isElement(_savedFocus) && document.contains(_savedFocus)) { _savedFocus.focus({ preventScroll: true }); } else { document.body.focus(); }`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in faqAccordion sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", leaving the state inconsistent (always false) for screen readers when the answer is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The open function in mobileNav reveals the drawer but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". The close function sets it to "false", causing screen readers to incorrectly report the menu as closed even when it is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function in dropdownMenu removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. This prevents assistive technology from detecting the open state, as the close function only manages the "false" state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error description via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. This means screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, despite the visual error message being present.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header visibility changes are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _onScroll function in stickyHeader hides and shows the header based on scroll direction without announcing these state changes. Users relying on screen readers are not informed when the navigation header disappears or reappears.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility links error messages to fields via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1084: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the navigation drawer visible but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2230: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` and before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function hides the mobile navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology receive no confirmation that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2253: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` immediately before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals dropdown panels but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern and prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2374: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` and before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Neither _openInstance() nor _closeInstance() announce state changes via the live region. Users do not receive feedback when dropdown menus open or close, creating uncertainty about the current UI state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2374: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` after setting aria-expanded. Line 2393: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');` after setting aria-expanded to false.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves the accessibility tree in an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4124: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` and before the animateHeight call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses FAQ panels without announcing the action to screen readers. Users are not informed when content is hidden, especially when closing via keyboard Escape key.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4144: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` immediately before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus management
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving into the drawer. Keyboard users cannot immediately access the newly revealed menu items, violating WCAG 2.4.3 focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2230: After opening the drawer, add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer and body but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance function sets it to "false", leaving the expanded state incorrect for assistive technology when the menu opens.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and updates classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", resulting in an inaccurate state announcement for screen reader users when an answer expands.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed on filter buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function toggles the CSS active class on filter tab buttons based on the selected category but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. This prevents assistive technology from identifying which filter is currently active among the button group.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions change visible content (showing/hiding panels) in response to user interaction but do not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips._onChipClick action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _onChipClick function removes a filter when a chip is clicked, updating the product grid, but does not announce this change to an aria-live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that a filter has been removed.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._addFile does not set aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The _addFile function validates the file and announces errors via liveRegion.announceAssertive if validation fails, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. This prevents assistive technology from marking the field as invalid programmatically.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply does not set aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: The _onApply function validates the discount code and announces invalidity via liveRegion.announceAssertive, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will announce the error text but the field state remains valid in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open/close does not toggle aria-hidden on drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the drawer visibility using CSS classes (is-open) which likely use transforms for off-canvas effects. However, the open and close functions do not toggle aria-hidden on the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels by toggling CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This leaves screen reader users unaware when dropdown menus open or close.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line in _closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open() and close() functions animate the panel height and manage the hidden attribute, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (item.trigger) to reflect the expanded or collapsed state of the answer panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line in close(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger acts as a two-state toggle button (open/closed). The code toggles the _open state variable and CSS classes, but never sets the aria-pressed attribute on the trigger element to indicate the active/pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` Line in close(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons (elements with [data-filter-value]) function as toggle buttons that activate specific categories. The code toggles the CSS active class but does not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _syncTabAriaStates(): Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds the error element to the field's aria-describedby, but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of the field to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markInvalid(): After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error reference from aria-describedby, but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". Fields may remain marked as invalid to assistive technologies even after being corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markValid(): Before or after the aria-describedby cleanup, add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Although the drawer is closed by default, the absence of the attribute prevents screen readers from understanding the component's initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After `_trigger.addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick);` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled (monthly/annual), the price values update visually (and animate), but the change is not announced to screen reader users via the live region system. Users may not be aware that the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`

errorMessage: Ollama HTTP 500: {"StatusCode":500,"Status":"500 Internal Server Error","error":"Internal Server Error (ref: 9c0325d0-3108-4020-bebd-908f69eaf5ac)"}

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields fail validation, aria-invalid is not set to "true" on the invalid input elements. When fields are corrected, aria-invalid is not cleared. Screen readers cannot convey the validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2704 (in _renderResult): When invalid, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. When valid, add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update their aria-expanded attribute when panels are opened or closed. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1764: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 1774: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the start of the _closeInstance function before `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation menu is closed, there is no announcement via the live region to inform screen reader users that the menu has closed and focus has returned to the trigger.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the close function before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1764: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');` after opening. Line 1774: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');` after closing.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results update not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied and the visible card count changes, the update is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the product grid has been filtered or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates the visual prices but does not announce the change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2390: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav aria-expanded not initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load when the menu is closed by default.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1697: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: toast notifications missing aria-hidden when dismissed
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications are hidden using CSS classes but aria-hidden is not explicitly set to "true", potentially leaving hidden notification content accessible to screen readers if the CSS uses opacity or transform instead of display:none.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1389: Add `setAttr(t, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after `addClass(t, CSS.TOAST_HIDE);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is not explicitly removed from the accessibility tree when closed. If the CSS uses transforms or positioning rather than display:none, screen readers may still access the hidden drawer content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1680: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` in the close function. Line 1666: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` in the open function after removing the hidden attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden management
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer (_drawer) visibility is toggled via CSS classes, but aria-hidden is not updated. If CSS does not use display: none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1673: _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line 1693: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function announces "Navigation menu opened" on open, but fails to announce "Navigation menu closed" on close. Screen reader users are not informed when the menu state changes to closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1693: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Users cannot detect if the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1843: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1853: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified when a menu expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1843: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
  Line 1853: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state to open but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2963: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item does not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed which answer is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2963: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded');
  Line 2990: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function associates an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1383: field.setAttribute(ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false". The field may remain flagged as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1396: field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the text and aria-label of the badge but does not announce the new count via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the filtered result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2596: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates prices and toggle states but does not announce the billing period change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual"). Screen reader users are not informed of the context change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2788: liveRegion.announce(period === 'annual' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function mounts the listbox but does not explicitly set aria-expanded="false" on the input initially. The state is undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2357: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: When field validation fails, the code adds error styling and aria-describedby pointing to the error message, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Conversely, when valid, it does not clear aria-invalid. This prevents screen readers from identifying invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _renderResult(): Inside `if (result.valid)`, add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`. Inside `else`, add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set, even though the menu is initially closed. This violates the requirement that the initial state of expandable content must be programmatically determinable.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing, the price values update visually, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realise the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line in setPeriod(): After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period });`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the visual change is not accompanied by an announcement to screen readers. This is particularly problematic when the dropdown opens without moving focus into it.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` at end of function. Line in _closeInstance(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` at end of function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons use aria-pressed to indicate the active filter state, but the JavaScript only toggles CSS classes and does not synchronise the aria-pressed attribute. This leaves screen readers unaware of which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _syncTabAriaStates(): Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden using the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded state is never updated in the open or close functions. This leaves screen reader users unaware of the dropdown’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line in _closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (the close function sets it to "false", but the open function omits setting it to "true"). This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox for the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not explicitly initialised to "false" when the component mounts. While the listbox is hidden, the input’s expanded state is ambiguous to assistive technologies until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init(): After `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes a filter chip, the filter is removed from the product grid, but this change is not announced. The user is not informed that the filter has been removed or that the product list has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _onChipClick(): Inside each case block after calling productFilter methods, add `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed');` (or use appropriate STRINGS key).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap and focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved into the drawer, focus is not trapped within the drawer while open, and when closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button. This makes the menu unusable for keyboard-only users.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): After `_lockScroll();`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` (and declare `_trap` assignment). Line in close(): Before `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The animated counter updates numerical values visually, but the final value (or the fact that the value has updated) is not announced to screen readers. Users relying on audio feedback will miss the statistics.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _animate(): Inside onComplete callback, after `el.textContent = _format(cfg.target, cfg);`, add `liveRegion.announce(el.textContent);`.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField missing aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (size or type), the error is announced via a live region, but the file input element itself is not marked with aria-invalid="true". This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as having an error when navigating the form.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _addFile(): Inside the `if (!validation.valid)` block, add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` before the return statement. Also add `removeAttr(binding.inputEl, ARIA.INVALID);` in a success handler or when clearing files.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded state updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger buttons (inst.trigger) do not have their aria-expanded attribute updated when the dropdown opens or closes. The register() function sets the initial state to false, but _openInstance() and _closeInstance() fail to toggle it, leaving screen reader users unaware of the panel's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1946: function _openInstance(inst) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }
  Line 1961: function _closeInstance(inst, returnFocus) { ... setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function sets the item state to open but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (item.trigger). The close() function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where screen readers are not informed when an answer expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2746: function open(item, announceToSR) { ... setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons ([data-filter-value]) are toggled via CSS class (is-active) in _syncTabAriaStates(), but their aria-pressed attribute is never updated. Screen reader users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2343: function _syncTabAriaStates() { ... setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive)); ... }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the aria-describedby reference but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute on the field. If a field was previously invalid (aria-invalid="true"), it remains invalid in the accessibility tree even after successful validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1335: function markValid(field, errorEl) { ... field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false'); ... }

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge() function updates the text content and aria-label of the product count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when the number of visible products changes due to filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2323: function _updateCountBadge(count) { ... liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} shown`); ... }

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates the UI to reflect the new billing period (monthly/annual) but does not announce this change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed when prices switch between billing cycles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: function setPeriod(period, animate) { ... liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual); ... }

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator missing aria-invalid attribute (JS-D)
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator marks fields as invalid using CSS classes and aria-describedby but never sets the aria-invalid attribute to "true" on invalid fields, preventing screen readers from identifying invalid inputs.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3050: In the `else` block (when !result.valid), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`. In the `if` block (when result.valid), add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding and removing CSS classes, but they never set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`. Line 1445: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap (JS-EXTRA)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function reveals an off-canvas navigation drawer but does not implement focus trapping, allowing keyboard focus to move outside the drawer to the underlying page while the drawer remains open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: After `open(announceToSR) {`, add `if (_drawer) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate(); }` and declare `_trap` variable at top of module. In close function, add `if (_trap) { _trap.deactivate(); _trap = null; }`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced (JS-C)
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function dynamically updates price displays across all plan cards when the billing period changes, but it does not announce this content change to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2440: After `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add `liveRegion.announce('Prices switched to ' + BILLING_PERIODS[period].label + ' billing');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons, leaving screen reader users unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _openInstance after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 1595: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _closeInstance before or after `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced (JS-C)
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function in productFilter dynamically shows and hides product cards and updates the count badge, but does not notify screen readers of the filter results or the new item count via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2670: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce('Showing ' + totalMatches + ' products');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in the pricing module updates the visual active state of billing period buttons (monthly/annual) but does not set aria-pressed to indicate which button is currently selected/pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: Inside the forEach loop over _periodBtns, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The _open and _close functions in siteSearch control the visibility of the suggestions listbox but fail to toggle aria-expanded on the search input element (which has role="combobox"), violating the ARIA disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _open function. Line 2260: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _close function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced (JS-C)
issuesFound.explanation: The open and close functions in faqAccordion expand and collapse FAQ panels but do not announce the state change (expanded/collapsed) or the item title to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2830: In open function, after `addClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' expanded');`. Line 2850: In close function, after `removeClass(item.el, CSS.open);`, add `liveRegion.announce(item.question + ' collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded (JS-E)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu user action not announced (JS-G)
issuesFound.explanation: User actions that open or close dropdown menus (via click or keyboard) update the DOM but lack a corresponding live-region announcement to inform screen reader users of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown opened');` in _openInstance. Line 1595: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing aria-hidden when closed (JS-F)

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing initial aria-expanded (JS-E)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state not announced (JS-C)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus via _openInstance and _closeInstance changes visible content without announcing the action to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1580: In _openInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`. Line 1595: In _closeInstance, add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed or aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function updates the visual active state on billing period buttons ([data-period]) and the toggle switch ([data-billing-toggle]) but does not update aria-pressed or aria-checked. The selected state is inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description but does not remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the input field. The invalid state may persist erroneously.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state not announced (JS-C)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible page content significantly but does not announce the navigation state change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: In open function, add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened');`. Line 1445: In close function, add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid (JS-D)
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils links an error message to a field using aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field, so assistive technologies cannot identify the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1050: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` at the end of the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded (JS-E)

issuesFound.title: mobileNav user action not announced (JS-G)
issuesFound.explanation: User actions toggling the mobile navigation drawer do not trigger live-region announcements, leaving screen reader users unaware that the navigation state has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1430: Add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation opened');` in open function. Line 1445: Add `liveRegion.announce('Navigation closed');` in close function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.open(), add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);. In mobileNav.close(), add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" state on the trigger button (.nav-toggle). Assistive technologies may announce the menu as expanded or have an indeterminate state on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.init(), after finding _trigger, add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not synced with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module toggles the drawer's visibility using CSS classes (is-open) without managing the aria-hidden attribute on the drawer element. When visually hidden via CSS, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, confusing screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In mobileNav.open(), add _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');. In mobileNav.close(), add _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not notified of the dropdown state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In _openInstance(), add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);. In _closeInstance(), add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger aria-expanded not initialised on registration
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. The initial state is missing for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In dropdownMenu.register(), after setting aria-controls, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open() and _close() functions show/hide the suggestion list but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (#search-input). This violates the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In siteSearch._open(), add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);. In siteSearch._close(), add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox aria-expanded not initialised on load
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function configures the combobox but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the input element. The initial collapsed state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In siteSearch.init(), after mounting the listbox, add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to false, causing a state mismatch where the trigger never reports as expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In faqAccordion.open(), add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In srUtils.markInvalid(), add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results count and empty state not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the product count badge and empty state visibility but does not announce these changes via a live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In applyFilters(), after updating the badge/empty state, add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions emit custom events but do not announce the expansion or collapse of the answer to screen readers via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In open(), add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);. In close(), add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);.
