issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link fields to error messages but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Conversely, markValid removes the describedby association but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute on valid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3476: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true') inside markInvalid after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);
  Line 3481: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false') inside markValid after the existing logic (or use removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID));

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but do not set the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to indicate the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1956: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true) inside _openInstance after inst.open = true;
  Line 1971: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false) inside _closeInstance after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable and references it in the close function, but the open function never creates or activates a focus trap. Keyboard focus can escape the drawer and move to background elements, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1854: Inside open(), after _lockScroll(); add: if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: Pricing changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes (monthly to annual), the price values update visually with animation, but there is no announcement to screen readers about the price change or the new billing period selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3165: After _updateSavingsMessage(period); add liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions animate the FAQ panels and update CSS classes but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2674: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true) inside the open function after item.open = true;
  Line 2698: Add setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false) inside the close function after item.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Product filter count updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function updates the text content of the count badge to show the number of visible products, but it does not announce this change via a live region, so screen reader users are not informed when filtering changes the result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2822: Add liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`) after _countBadge.textContent = String(count);

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The play() and pause() functions in testimonialsCarousel update the _playing state and change the aria-label of the play/pause button, but they do not set aria-pressed to indicate whether the button is currently in the "pressed" (playing) state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2937: Add setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, true) inside play() after _playing = true;
  Line 2950: Add setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, false) inside pause() after _playing = false;

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function updates the visual active state of billing period buttons (monthly/quarterly/annual) using CSS classes but does not set aria-pressed to indicate which button is currently selected in the toggle button group.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3181: Add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive) inside the forEach loop after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);

issuesFound.title: Site search input does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open() and _close() functions manage the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not toggle the aria-expanded attribute on the search input element to indicate whether the suggestions are displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2407: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true) inside _open after _searchState.open = true;
  Line 2416: Add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false) inside _close after _searchState.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function sets up aria-controls, aria-haspopup, and aria-autocomplete on the search input, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" to indicate that the suggestion list is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: After setAttr(_inputEl, 'autocomplete', 'off'); add setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not initialise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "false" when the page loads and the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1938: After _initialised = true; add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function identifies the trigger button (_trigger) and the drawer element (_drawer) but does not establish an aria-controls relationship between them. The trigger should reference the drawer ID via aria-controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1928: After _drawer is assigned, add: const drawerId = srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, drawerId);

issuesFound.title: Pricing switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (_toggleEl) has role="switch" but the code never sets the aria-checked attribute to reflect whether the switch is in the "year" (checked) or "month" (unchecked) state. The switch role requires aria-checked to indicate its state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3220: Add setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, next === 'year') inside _onToggleClick after determining the next period;
  Line 3160: Add setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, period === 'year') inside setPeriod to ensure initial state is correct;

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not trigger a live region announcement to inform screen reader users that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1956: Add liveRegion.announce(`Menu opened`) inside _openInstance;
  Line 1971: Add liveRegion.announce(`Menu closed`) inside _closeInstance;

issuesFound.title: Breadcrumb current page not marked with aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The _processNav function in breadcrumbHelpers identifies the last breadcrumb item as the current page (commented as "Mark the last item as the current page") but the actual code to set aria-current="page" is missing. The last link is not marked as the current page for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2126: After const lastLink = qs('a', lastItem); add: if (lastLink) lastLink.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page'); else lastItem.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page');

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init function sets up the play button but does not explicitly initialize aria-pressed based on the initial _playing state. If the carousel auto-plays, the button should reflect that it can be paused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2920: if (_playBtn) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(CONFIG.carousel && CONFIG.carousel.autoPlay !== false)); }

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the visual change is not announced to screen readers. Users navigating with assistive technology are not informed that new content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2674: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`) inside open() after the panel is shown;
  Line 2698: Add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`) inside close() after the panel is hidden;

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed when the navigation menu becomes available or when it is dismissed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1854: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened') inside open();
  Line 1876: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed') inside close();

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed when the answer expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2673: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer toggle missing aria-hidden management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the drawer using CSS classes but do not update aria-hidden. If the CSS hides the element visually without display:none, it remains in the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1505: addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');
  Line 1518: removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing aria-expanded state update
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and _close functions manage the suggestion list visibility but do not update aria-expanded on the search input element. The input has role="combobox" which requires this attribute to reflect the list state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1935: _searchState.open = true; setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
  Line 1945: _searchState.open = false; setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu registration missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function initializes the panel as hidden but does not set the trigger's aria-expanded attribute to "false". Assistive technologies may announce the trigger as expanded by default if the attribute is absent.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1775: panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Form validation markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1385: addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Form validation markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid function removes the error description but fails to set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain announced as invalid even after the user corrects the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1395: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: Product filter apply function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid and count badge dynamically but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that the content has changed after filtering.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', { count: totalMatches, state: Object.assign({}, s) }); liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: Pricing setPeriod function missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price text dynamically across all plan cards but does not announce the billing period change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have switched between monthly and annual.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2530: emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', { from: prev, to: period }); liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animator missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates the textContent of numeric counters dynamically. There is no live region announcement to inform screen reader users that the statistics have finished loading or updating.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3580: el.textContent = _format(cfg.target, cfg); liveRegion.announce(`Statistic updated to ${el.textContent}`);

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function missing screen reader announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but relies on the announceToSR parameter which is not consistently passed or utilized for the specific content change. A dedicated live region announcement ensures the expansion is spoken.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2673: item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing aria-controls initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function mounts the listbox and wires ARIA attributes, but the aria-controls attribute is set in suggestionListRenderer.mount. If the listbox ID generation fails or is delayed, the association is broken. Ensure aria-controls is set reliably in init.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1910: const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl); setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-controls', listbox.id);

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the invalid prop is true, the component visually styles the input as an error but does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Slides that are not currently visible remain in the accessibility tree, causing screen readers to read content from hidden slides and confusing users about the current carousel position.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the div with className 'testimonial-slide'.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons function as a toggle group indicating the active filter category but do not communicate the pressed state to assistive technology, leaving screen reader users unaware of which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to each button.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles a disclosure panel but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the answer content is currently displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button props.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup incorrect error prop usage
issuesFound.explanation: FormGroup expects errorMessage prop but receives error. The error message will not render, and screen readers will not announce validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Change error={email.state.error} to errorMessage={email.state.error} and add errorId="newsletter-email-error".

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm server error message rendering failure
issuesFound.explanation: The ErrorMessage component expects a message prop, but children text is passed instead. Since the component checks for message prop truthiness and returns null otherwise, the error message will never display.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Replace <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" role="alert">Something went wrong...</ErrorMessage> with <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-status" message="Something went wrong. Please try again in a moment." />

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons in FilterTabs function as a toggle group but lack aria-pressed to indicate which category is currently active. Assistive technology users cannot identify the selected filter state. The activeFilter state variable should be mirrored in the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 858: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>

issuesFound.title: Testimonial carousel slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible slides in the TestimonialsCarousel are visually hidden via CSS but remain in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users will hear all testimonials instead of just the active one. The aria-hidden attribute must conditionally hide inactive slides.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1208: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling but fails to pass it to the underlying input element as aria-invalid. Assistive technology users are not notified when the field is in an error state. This affects all instances including NewsletterForm and ContactForm.
issuesFound.fix: Line 363: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the input is in an error state, the invalid prop only applies a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 854: <input ... aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.fix: Line 606: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons toggle the active category state visually but lack aria-pressed. Assistive technology users cannot identify which filter is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1075: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing toggle button reflects selection state via class names only. It lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1363: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button reflects selection state via class names only. It lacks aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1371: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton with only an icon character. It fails to pass the required aria-label prop, leaving the button without an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 707: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in MobileNav uses IconButton but does not provide an aria-label. Screen reader users encounter an unnamed button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: <IconButton onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close" aria-label="Close navigation panel">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The previous testimonial button uses IconButton without an aria-label. The icon character alone does not provide an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1233: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev" aria-label="Previous testimonial">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next IconButton missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The next testimonial button uses IconButton without an aria-label. The icon character alone does not provide an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1238: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next" aria-label="Next testimonial">

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but only applies a CSS class. It fails to set the aria-invalid attribute on the native input element, so assistive technology is not notified of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 825: <input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component supports an isLoading state but does not set aria-busy on the button element. Assistive technology users are not informed that the button is busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk indicating a required field is rendered as text without aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce the asterisk character unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix: Line 804: *

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in DesktopNav use a current prop to apply a visual class but lack aria-current="page". Assistive technology users cannot identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 559: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in MobileNav use a current prop to apply a visual class but lack aria-current="page". Assistive technology users cannot identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 624: <a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav landmarks on the page, this one is not uniquely identifiable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 736: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav landmarks on the page, this one is not uniquely identifiable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 547: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section contains a heading but the section element itself lacks aria-labelledby referencing it. The landmark region is not properly named for navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 927: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard article element contains a heading but lacks aria-labelledby referencing it. The card region is not properly named for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar clone ul missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second ul in IntegrationsBar is a visual clone for the marquee effect but is not hidden from assistive technology. This causes duplicate content to be announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1033: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">
